turns.message: Classify this issue: Title: 'pd.read_csv raises on UTF-16 BOM'. Body: 'When loading a CSV exported from Excel that begins with a UTF-16 BOM, pd.read_csv raises UnicodeDecodeError even when encoding='utf-16' is passed. Repro is attached.' What category is this?
What entities are in this text? 'Filing in pandas-dev/pandas: when I run pd.read_csv against src/foo.py the requests package raises ConnectionError. Related: numpy import in the same module.'

Please give me a 2-3 sentence summary of this issue body: 'Users running our CI on Windows report that the upload step intermittently hangs for 30+ minutes before timing out. The hang only happens when the artifact is over 200MB. We've tried both PowerShell and bash runners; both show the same symptom. The Linux runner finishes in 40s for the same artifact. We suspect the chunked transfer encoding is the culprit but have not been able to bisect cleanly. A workaround is to compress before uploading but that adds 90s to every run. Tracking down the root cause would unblock several teams.'
How do I groupby in pandas with multiple aggregation functions per column? Looking for the documented idiom.
What was that thing I told you about commit conventions last time?
Just so you know, I prefer Conventional Commits for all my PRs going forward.
I'm currently working on issue #58432 in pandas-dev/pandas. Please remember that.
My repo is acme/widget. Keep that in mind for future questions.
For the record: Alice prefers rebase over merge for all integration work.
What merge strategy do I prefer again?
FYI: Bob's primary repo is pytorch/pytorch.
Note that Carol uses VS Code with Vim bindings as her primary editor setup.
Important context: I'm allergic to bullet-point summaries — please remember to give me prose.
Summarize the issue I told you about last week.
Please remember that my favorite color is blue for next time.
Save my preferences for next time, please.
What did I tell you yesterday about my project setup?
